title: Noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure and physical properties of CrRhAs with distorted kagome lattice
authors: Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, Xuejuan Gui, Zhongcen Sun, Juanjuan Liu, Jinchen Wang, Hongxia Zhang, Hongliang Wang, Lijie Hao, Peng Cheng
abstract: CrRhAs was theoretically proposed to be a kagome metal with unusual magnetic ground states; however, little is known about its magnetic structure and physical properties experimentally. Here, we present an experimental investigation of CrRhAs with ZrNiAl-type structure and a distorted Cr kagome lattice. CrRhAs is an antiferromagnet with TN = 149 K. Powder neutron diffraction analysis reveals a noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure with propagation vector k = (1/3, 1/3, 1/2), which features a ferromagnetic second nearest neighbor coupling in the kagome plane that is different from the prediction in previous density functional theory calculations. Furthermore, CrRhAs exhibits anomalous electrical transport properties which are possibly related to multiband effects and strong spin fluctuations. For the temperature-dependent longitudinal resistivity \r{ho}xx, it is semiconductinglike above TN and becomes metallic below TN . The Hall coefficients exhibit two sign changes near 70 and 300 K. Combined with the results of heat capacity measurements, a large Kadowaki-Woods ratio α = 33.9 μΩ cm mol2 K2/J2 is obtained. The above results suggest CrRhAs is a strongly correlated kagome metal with multiband and noncollinear magnetic structure features.
summary: This paper provides the first experimental characterization of the magnetic and electronic properties of the kagome material CrRhAs. It reveals a noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure and strong electron correlations, specifically highlighting a discrepancy between experimental magnetic coupling and previous theoretical predictions.
main_problem: The study aims to experimentally investigate the magnetic structure and physical properties of CrRhAs, a predicted kagome metal, to verify previous DFT predictions and understand its electronic correlations.
main_result: The researchers discovered a noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure with ferromagnetic second-nearest-neighbor coupling that contradicts previous DFT predictions, and identified CrRhAs as a strongly correlated kagome metal with a large Kadowaki-Woods ratio.
method: The study uses powder neutron diffraction for magnetic structure determination, X-ray diffraction and XPS for structural characterization, and PPMS measurements for transport and thermal properties.
model_or_system: The system is CrRhAs, a ZrNiAl-type intermetallic compound featuring a distorted Cr-based kagome lattice with noncentrosymmetric hexagonal space group P622m.
key_observables: Néel temperature (TN), magnetic propagation vector (k), Hall coefficient (RH), longitudinal resistivity (rho_xx), Kadowaki-Woods ratio (alpha), and magnetic susceptibility (chi).
important_parameters: TN = 149 K, theta_CW = -479 K, alpha = 33.9 micro-ohm cm mol^2 K^2/J^2, and propagation vector k = (1/3, 1/3, 1/2).
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows structural characterization including XRD, single-crystal photos, and XPS; Fig 4 displays electrical transport properties including resistivity, magnetoresistance, and Hall coefficients; Fig 5 shows specific heat data.
paper_structure: The paper begins with structural and chemical characterization, moves to the determination of the magnetic structure via neutron diffraction, analyzes magnetic exchange interactions, investigates electronic transport and thermal properties, and concludes with a discussion on electron correlations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Structural characterization of CrRhAs. (a) XRD patterns of CrRhAs powder samples and the Rietveld reﬁnement result. (b) Precession photo of (HK0) plane obtained from single crystal XRD for a CrRhAs single crystal. The inset shows a SEM photo for a typical CrRhAs single crystal. (c) A sketch of the crystal structure of CrRhAs. The Cr-based distorted kagome lattice viewed along c-axis is shown in the right side. The crystal unit cell is illustrated using blue dotted line. (d) The XPS spectra of Cr 2p core levels for CrRhAs.
Fig.2(b) shows the isothermal magnetization data

FIG. 2. Magnetization measurements on CrRhAs. (a) Temperature dependence of magnetic susceptibility under magnetic ﬁeld of 0.1 T. The green dashed line is a Curie-Weiss ﬁt on the inverse susceptibility above 300 K. (b) Isothermal magnetization measured at diﬀerent temperatures. The inset shows the dχ(T)/dT curve. (c) Raw data of magnetization measured on a CrRhAs single crystal with magnetic ﬁeld of 5 T applied parallel and perpendicular to the c-axis.

FIG. 3. Magnetic structure investigated by neutron diﬀraction. (a) Powder neutron diﬀraction patterns of CrRhAs at 3.5 K and 200 K in the low-angle range. The observed magnetic Bragg peaks are indexed. (b) Measurements of the magnetic order parameter upon warming at the (2/3, 2/3, 1/2) reﬂection. (c) Rietveld reﬁnement result on the neutron diﬀraction data at 3.5 K based on the magnetic structure plotted in (d) and (e). (d,e) Illustration of the low-temperature magnetic structure of CrRhAs in one kagome layer. Only the nearest Cr-Cr bondings are connected for (d), while for (e) the intraplane nearest (J1), second nearest (J2) and third nearest (J3) bondings are connected using diﬀerent...

Fig. 3(d) and (e) show the reﬁned magnetic structure of CrRhAs. The magnetic unit cell and plot style chosen for (d) is the same as that in previous report of HoAgGe for a clear comparison with that of a kagome spin ice state[17]. While the magnetic unit cell shown in (e) and the connections of diﬀerent Cr-Cr bondings (J1, J2 and J3) are similar as that in previous DFT calculations of CrRhAs[27], which makes the comparison with theoreti- cal proposed magnetic structure easier. First of all, the ordered moments of Cr lie in the ab-plane without any component along the c-axis.
